# Project 2: System Call Monitoring Tool

## 📝 Objective
Understand how high-level C standard I/O wrappers interact with the Linux kernel's Virtual File System (VFS). This project utilizes `strace` to audit low-level system calls such as `execve`, `mmap`, `openat`, `read`, and `write`.

## 📂 Files Included
* `file_tool.c`: A C program that creates, writes to, and reads from a text log file.
* `runtime_log.txt`: The text file generated by the program during execution.
* `strace_output.txt`: The raw kernel interaction audit log generated by `strace`.
* `README.md`: Project documentation.

## 🚀 Build & Trace Instructions
Navigate to this directory in your terminal and compile the source code:
```bash
gcc -o file_tool file_tool.c

Execute the binary while routing system calls to an output file using strace:

Bash
strace -o strace_output.txt ./file_tool
View the generated kernel logs to observe the system calls:

Bash
cat strace_output.txt
